skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices". Fetches latest Vercel guidelines and checks files against all rules.
skill.summary: Automated UI code review using Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines with accessibility checks
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 44 static analysis findings are false positives from documentation and metadata. External command detections are markdown code blocks, network findings are legitimate documentation URLs, filesystem detections are relative path references in documentation, and cryptographic detections are YAML metadata fields. No malicious intent or security risks found.

content.user_title: Review UI Code for Web Standards Compliance
content.value_statement: Ensure your UI code follows industry best practices and accessibility standards. This skill automatically checks your components against Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines and reports violations with specific line numbers and fix suggestions.
actual_capabilities: Fetch latest Vercel Web Interface Guidelines dynamically
Review React, Vue, Svelte, HTML, CSS, and TypeScript files
Report violations in file:line format with specific locations
Provide fix suggestions for each guideline violation
Support single file or glob pattern reviews
limitations: Cannot execute code or run applications
Requires files to be available in the workspace
Guidelines accuracy depends on external Vercel repository
May report false positives on complex UI patterns
Does not replace manual accessibility testing with screen readers

use_cases.title: Pre-commit UI Review
use_cases.description: Run this skill before committing UI changes to catch accessibility and design pattern violations early in development

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audit
use_cases.description: Audit existing components for WCAG compliance when preparing for accessibility reviews or compliance requirements

use_cases.title: Code Quality Check
use_cases.description: Review pull requests or component libraries to ensure consistent design patterns across the entire codebase

prompt_templates.title: Single Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my UI component at src/components/Button.tsx for Web Interface Guidelines compliance
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a single component before committing changes

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check accessibility of all components in src/components/**/*.tsx against WCAG guidelines
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive accessibility audit of a component directory

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my entire UI codebase for design pattern violations and report all findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete design system audit for quality assurance

prompt_templates.title: Specific Pattern Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit src/pages/*.tsx for modal and dialog patterns, checking focus management and aria attributes
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted review of specific UI patterns across multiple files

output_examples.output:
src/components/Button.tsx:15 - Button should have aria-label for icon-only buttons
src/components/Button.tsx:32 - Focus indicator missing (contrast ratio 2.1:1 below 3:1 minimum)
src/components/Button.tsx:45 - Missing keyboard event handler for click action

output_examples.input: Check my modal components in src/components/Modal.tsx
output_examples.output:
src/components/Modal.tsx:12 - Modal should trap focus within itself when open
src/components/Modal.tsx:28 - Missing aria-modal="true" attribute
src/components/Modal.tsx:35 - Close button lacks accessible description
src/components/Modal.tsx:42 - Body scroll not prevented when modal is open

src/pages/Home.tsx:8 - Main content should be wrapped in <main> element
src/pages/Home.tsx:15 - Use <nav> instead of <div> for navigation section
src/pages/Home.tsx:22 - Heading levels skip from h2 to h4 (missing h3)
src/pages/Home.tsx:38 - Images missing alt attributes

best_practices: Provide specific file paths or glob patterns rather than asking for generic reviews
Run reviews incrementally on small batches of files to avoid context overflow
Use findings as a starting point for manual accessibility testing with real users
Combine with automated testing tools for comprehensive quality assurance
Review findings during pull request process to catch issues before merge
anti_patterns: Running on entire large codebases in single review (causes context overflow and incomplete analysis)
Treating automated findings as complete accessibility testing (still need manual testing)
Ignoring fix suggestions (they explain the reasoning behind each violation)
Running only once during development (guidelines change and code evolves)
Using without understanding basic accessibility principles (findings need context)

faq.question: What guidelines does this skill check against?
faq.answer: This skill fetches and applies Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines, which cover accessibility, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, focus management, and modern web design best practices.

faq.question: Does this replace manual accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. Automated checks catch many issues but cannot replace testing with screen readers, keyboard navigation, and real users. Use this as part of a broader accessibility strategy.

faq.question: What file types can I review?
faq.answer: You can review React, Vue, Svelte, HTML, CSS/SCSS, TypeScript, and JavaScript files. The skill analyzes markup, structure, and common UI patterns.

faq.question: How often are the guidelines updated?
faq.answer: The skill fetches the latest guidelines from Vercel's repository on every review, ensuring you always check against the most current rules.

faq.question: Can I review multiple files at once?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a glob pattern like src/**/*.tsx to review multiple files. For large codebases, review smaller batches to avoid context overflow.

faq.question: What if I disagree with a finding?
faq.answer: Some findings may be context-specific. Review the suggestion against your use case. If it is a false positive, document the exception with a comment explaining the deviation.
